response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior full-stack developer with strong web/backend credentials, but the resume shows almost none of the role's hard requirements: no native Japanese, no Linux kernel modification experience, no embedded firmware or LAN-switch background, and no demonstrated security-protocol work (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief National Instruments stint over a decade ago and an academic project, far from the 'strong C for UNIX' bar. The profile fits a TypeScript/Node web role, not embedded switch security.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development with senior/staff titles
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, open-source contributions
Broad polyglot exposure including some low-level work (DLang async I/O, C++ wave field synthesis)
concerns: No native (or any) Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement
No Linux kernel investigation/modification or embedded firmware experience
No demonstrated network-security, L2/L3 switching, or MACsec/802.1X background; C experience is minimal and dated
